name: Rhode Island Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Rhode Island - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

address.description: Rhode Island Department of Health, 3 Capitol Hill, Providence, RI 02908-5097

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Rhode Island SLP license cost?
Is Rhode Island part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Rhode Island allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Rhode Island have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Rhode Island. To work in Rhode Island, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Rhode Island?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Rhode Island while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
